content.method: Fiscal incidence analysis using per-pupil capitation formulas and school-level budget data comparing feeder versus selective-track schools; cost-effectiveness modeling of compensatory funding scaled to the documented disadvantaged-skill gap (12.1% vs 16.3% EU average), with sensitivity analysis under the disputed government/civil-society statistics baselines.
content.question: What is the fiscal magnitude of capitation funding following students into 6/8-year gimnaziums relative to losses at feeder primary schools, and would targeted compensatory funding (T4) plausibly offset the disadvantaged-achievement gap at a cost proportionate to Hungary's overall education budget?
content.why_it_matters: This assesses whether T4's non-structural approach can deliver meaningful equity gains at feasible cost, or whether it is a bounded palliative that cannot substitute for structural reform, informing resource allocation decisions independent of whether structural reform proceeds.
